commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the border went out toward the sea by Michmethah on the north side; and the border turned about eastward unto Taanathshiloh, and passed by it on the east to Janoah; Looketh to the north The meaning is, that the border went towards the north, by Machmethath; and then turned eastward to Thanathselo. (Challoner) Borders. It should be terminus. "The border turneth eastward "Septuagint. (Bonfrere) Janoe, twelve miles east of Sichem. (Eusebius) (4 Kings xv.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Looketh to the north: The meaning is, that the border went towards the north, by Machmethath; and then turned eastward to Thanath-selo.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
